name: Klarna
description_short: Buy now, pay later in 4 interest-free installments. 150M+ users. Now reports on-time payments to credit bureaus.
description_long:
Klarna is a Swedish fintech company and one of the largest buy now, pay later (BNPL) providers globally, serving over 150 million users and 500,000 merchants. Founded in 2005 in Stockholm, Klarna is licensed as a bank in Europe and operates in the US as a payment platform.

Klarna offers several payment options: Pay in 4 (split any purchase into 4 interest-free payments over 6 weeks), Pay in 30 Days (try before you buy with 30 days to pay), and Klarna Financing (longer-term installment plans for larger purchases, subject to credit approval and interest).

Starting in 2024, Klarna began reporting on-time Pay in 4 payments to TransUnion, meaning consistent BNPL usage can now help build your credit history. Late payments may also be reported, so it's important to stay current.

The Klarna app includes price drop alerts, a wish list, one-click checkout across thousands of retailers, and a loyalty program (Klarna Rewards). There are no fees for Pay in 4 as long as you make payments on time — late fees apply for missed payments.
pros: Now reports to TransUnion — builds credit
No hard credit pull for Pay in 4
cons: Late fees for missed Pay in 4 payments
Only reports to TransUnion (not all 3 bureaus)
best_for: Building credit through on-time BNPL payments
Shopping with price tracking and rewards
diagnosis: Klarna is a buy-now-pay-later (BNPL) platform, fundamentally a payment flexibility tool at checkout—not a traditional credit product. While it reports payment history to TransUnion (added 2024), credit-building is a secondary benefit, not the core service. Does not fit CreditDoc lending categories (credit repair, personal loans, debt relief, etc.). Included in directory as reference only for users seeking alternative payment methods.
